articles.headline: FDA Issues Draft Guidance on Genome Editing Safety Standards to Advance Gene Therapy Development
articles.summary: FDA released draft guidance establishing standardized safety assessment methods for genome editing therapies, specifically focusing on next-generation sequencing approaches to evaluate off-target editing risks. The guidance covers both ex vivo and in vivo gene editing products and provides specific recommendations on sequencing strategies, sample selection, and reporting requirements for IND and BLA submissions. This builds on FDA's broader framework for accelerating individualized therapies for ultra-rare diseases launched in February.
articles.why_it_matters: Regulatory clarity on genome editing safety standards could reduce development risk and accelerate approval timelines for gene therapy companies, potentially impacting valuations across the CRISPR and gene editing sector.

articles.headline: Frontier AI's Economic Engine
articles.summary: Analysis of enterprise AI adoption showing coding applications have achieved the strongest product-market fit, far exceeding other use cases. The piece examines why software development leads AI adoption (verifiable outcomes, abundant training data, tractable problem domain) and explores the strategic implications for companies like Anthropic that have focused on this market.
articles.why_it_matters: Offers a data-driven framework for evaluating where AI delivers measurable ROI versus hype, particularly valuable for assessing AI infrastructure investments and understanding competitive dynamics in the frontier AI market.
